commands.review_summary:
Adds nonzero active-lease and monthly USD guardrail vars to production and preview Worker wrangler config, plus a Vitest regression test that keeps those vars set.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than live deployment: current main's `worker/wrangler.jsonc` does not set the cost-cap vars, while `worker/src/usage.ts` already reads and enforces them when present. I did not run or deploy the Worker in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Needs maintainer confirmation of cap values and deployment timing; there is no narrow code defect for an automated repair lane.

target.title: fix: enable broker cost cap defaults
target.body:
Summary:
- add explicit coordinator active-lease and monthly USD caps to the checked-in Worker deploy config
- add a Worker config regression test so production and preview vars cannot silently lose those caps

Verification:
- npm ci --prefix worker
- npm test --prefix worker -- wrangler-config
- npm run format:check --prefix worker
- npm run lint --prefix worker
- npm run check --prefix worker
- npm run build --prefix worker
- npm test --prefix worker
- git diff --check

Deployment note:
- this branch changes the deploy config, but production still needs a Worker deploy with Cloudflare auth before crabbox usage reports nonzero limits
- current local shell has no Cloudflare or Azure auth, so I did not deploy
target.author: vincentkoc
